name: Thomas of Maleum and Acacius mentioned in "The Ladder"
kontakia: (Tone 4) With courage you pushed on to victory,* having been inflamed with divine love.* You offended the mortal king and spurned the beauties of this earth.* Ending your days on the mountain of Maleum,* you stepped up from it into heaven itself.* O Thomas, pray unceasingly for all of us.

(Tone 2) From childhood, most wise and venerable Acacius,* you followed after Christ, forsaking the world.* Imitating his willing meekness, you overcame the proud torturer.* Now pray unceasingly for all of us.
